title: Lepidaploa cleocalderonae
intro: Lepidaploa cleocalderonae is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and pain, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Lepidaploa cleocalderonae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. Lepidaploa cleocalderonae is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, menstrual cramps, and other inflammatory disorders.
taxonomy: Lepidaploa cleocalderonae is classified within the family Asteraceae and the genus Lepidaploa. The genus Lepidaploa is a part of the subfamily Cichorioideae. It belongs to the order Asterales, which comprises various flowering plants, including daisies and sunflowers. Lepidaploa cleocalderonae's classification also includes the class Magnoliopsida and the kingdom Plantae. This classification system helps to identify and understand the plant's relationships with other species.
